menu.title: Stickman Runner
menu.campaignsEmpty: No campaigns yet: create one in the Level Editor.
game.instructions: Arrows / A-D to move, Up or Space to jump
pause.title: Paused
editor.title: Level Editor
editor.section.heading: Section
editor.section.resizeHint: Resizing keeps your existing grid/entities: new rows are added above (so the ground row stays anchored at the bottom) and new columns at the right. Shrinking past painted content deletes it.
editor.levelSequence.heading: Level sequence
editor.levelSequence.hint: Drop the section files into assets/levels/sections/ and this level's file into assets/levels/levels/ (as <level id>.json). Then add "<level id>" to the sequence array in assets/levels/levels.json (if it isn't there already) to play it in-game.
editor.campaign.heading: Campaign
editor.campaign.hint: Levels here must each have exactly one spawn and one goal, summed across their sections: invalid ones show red. Saved campaigns appear in the game's main menu.
editor.tiles.layerHint: Foreground holds interactable Ground/Hazard tiles and entities: this is what the player collides with. Background holds purely decorative ground-equivalent sprites behind the action; it never collides and can't hold entities.
editor.tiles.fgHint: Drag to paint. The highlighted bottom row is the ground baseline every section anchors to. Each brush below is its own sprite/color: add more in the Tileset section to paint several distinct-looking Ground/Hazard tiles in one section.
editor.tiles.bgHint: Drag to paint decorative background tiles. These sit behind everything else and never block or collide with the player.
editor.tiles.tilesetHint: Click a type to edit its color/sprite. "+ Add variant" makes a new brush with its own look (e.g. a second Ground sprite) that shows up as its own tool above and in the Type picker on a selected platform below. Each section keeps its own tileset, so different sections can look completely different even reused across levels.
editor.entities.heading: Entities
editor.entities.hint: Click an empty cell to place the selected entity (Spawn/Goal are singletons: placing a new one moves the old; Moving/enemies allow any number). Already-placed entities and platform tiles are listed in the right sidebar: select one there (or click its marker/tile here) to edit its properties. Selecting this tab switches the Tiles tab back to the Foreground layer, since entities can't live on the Background layer.
editor.assets.heading: Assets
editor.assets.hint: Assets belong to the campaign selected below in the Campaign panel: uploading one here bundles it with that campaign, not shared with any other. Kind (image vs. audio) is detected automatically from the file you pick.
editor.assets.imagesEmpty: No images yet: upload a PNG/JPEG (≤128KB) file.
editor.assets.audioEmpty: No audio yet: upload an MP3/OGG (≤1MB) file.
editor.assets.selectCampaignFirst: Select or create a campaign in the Campaign panel first.
editor.rightSidebar.heading: Entities & Platforms
editor.rightSidebar.entitiesEmpty: None placed yet: use the Entities tools on the left.
editor.rightSidebar.platformsEmpty: None placed yet: paint Ground/Hazard on the left.
editor.rightSidebar.backgroundEmpty: None placed yet: switch to the Background layer and paint on the left.
editor.rightSidebar.inspectorEmptyHint: Click an item above (or a marker/tile on the grid) to view and edit its properties.
editor.alert.noVariantSlots: No more tile-variant slots available for this section.
editor.confirm.removeVariant: Remove this variant? Any tiles painted with it will be cleared back to empty.
editor.alert.invalidSectionJson: That JSON does not look like a valid section file.
editor.confirm.newBlankSection: Start a new blank section? Unsaved changes will be lost.
editor.hint.noSpriteTextures: No sprite textures available yet: add one to assets/images/platform-textures.json.
editor.alert.indexedDbUnavailable: IndexedDB is not available in this browser.
editor.confirm.overwriteSuffix: already exists in your library: overwrite it?
editor.confirm.deleteSectionSuffix: from the library? Levels referencing it will show an error.
editor.confirm.deleteLevelSuffix: from the library? Campaigns referencing it will show an error.
editor.confirm.deleteCampaignSuffix: This removes the campaign and its uploaded assets, but not its levels/sections (those stay in the library).
editor.alert.selectCampaignFirst: Select or create a campaign first.
editor.inspector.placementHint: Click a cell on the grid to place this waypoint.
editor.tileMode.stretchHint: One image stretched to cover the whole tile: simple, but distorts a sprite that isn’t naturally that shape.
editor.tileMode.repeatHint: Tiles the sprite at its native size, repeating across the area: no stretching, but seams can show on odd sizes.
editor.tileMode.maximiseHint: Finds the largest rectangular block(s) within this connected same-tile area and stretches each one individually: good for big or irregularly-shaped areas (e.g. a staircase of the same tile) since each piece stays closer to square.
